growth_picks.name: Newmont Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 45.8% — well above 30% pass threshold; reflects gold-price tailwind and post-merger production.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 67.36% — clears the 65% pass bar, exceptional for a miner.

rule_of_40.detail: 45.8 + 61.4 = 107.2, far above 50 — top-tier capital efficiency for a commodity producer.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 78.6% > revenue growth 45.8% — clear operating leverage on price.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 25.83% — above the 20% pass bar with low debt-to-equity (15.76).

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.03% (<5%) AND declining 10.55% — clean supply, no overhang.

ai_exposure.detail: Gold mining is largely insulated from AI; minor opex tailwind from AI-assisted exploration/maintenance, but not a thesis driver.

deal_radar.assessment: Net cash position implied (cash $8.78B vs debt $5.53B); FCF $9.8B funds buybacks/dividend. Low payout ratio 13.1% leaves room for capital returns.

catalysts_general: Trading 19.18% below 52-week high while analyst mean target $144.01 implies 32.1% upside.
Forward P/E 9.62 vs trailing 14.14 — earnings expansion priced cheap.
Short interest dropped 10.55% MoM with only 1.67 days-to-cover — bears stepping aside.
Free cash flow $9.8B vs $116B market cap = ~8.4% FCF yield.
Analyst distribution: 17 buy/strong-buy vs 4 hold-or-worse out of 21; rec mean 1.62.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Gold pulls back 10%; reverts to 50-day SMA region.
Gold correction to ~$2,800; multiple compresses on cyclical fears.
Hard recession + gold to $2,500; sentiment break, prior-year support.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below 50-day SMA ($112.93) and round-number support; offers ~44% to mean target with same fundamentals.

comparables.note: Senior gold peer; typically commands premium to NEM on operational consistency.
Barrick — closest peer; NEM's 9.6x fwd P/E historically discounts the group.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Newmont passes all six growth-framework criteria with a forward P/E under 10 — a rare combination. Gold cycle is the macro risk, but ROE 25.8%, FCF $9.8B, and a clean short-interest profile make it the cleanest BUY in this set on fundamentals.

growth_picks.name: Applovin Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 65.9% — far above 30% pass threshold.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 87.86% — software-like profile, clears 65% bar comfortably.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 84.7% > revenue growth 65.9% — operating leverage intact.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 212.95% — extreme, partly due to thin book value ($6.31) and 171.8 D/E; clears bar nominally but quality is flattered by leverage.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 4.26% (<5%) AND declining 5.03% — bears trimming.

ai_exposure.detail: Axon AI ad-targeting engine is the core growth driver; AdTech leverage on AI inference improvements is direct revenue translation.

deal_radar.assessment: Cash $2.49B vs debt $3.67B — modest net debt; FCF $2.7B easily services it. High D/E (171.8) is a watchpoint but covered by FCF profile.

catalysts_general: Down 35.88% from 52-week high but 21d/50d SMAs ($449/$442) flipping bullish vs spot.
Analyst mean target $638.50 implies 33.5% upside.
Forward P/E 22.48 vs trailing 47.62 — earnings expected to roughly double y/y.
PEG 1.33 reasonable given 65.9% growth.
Beta 2.37 — high-volatility name; entries on dips materially improve risk/reward.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Tape pullback to 200-day region; growth narrative intact.
Analyst low target; ad-tech multiple compression on macro risk-off.
Retest 52-week low on AI ad-spend miss or accounting concerns.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Slight discount to current 20d SMA ($449) and forward P/E ~20x — buys momentum at attractive risk/reward with framework fully intact.

comparables.note: Trade Desk — direct ad-tech peer; APP trades cheaper despite faster growth.
Different scale but similar AI-ads thesis; APP higher growth, smaller, more volatile.

growth_picks.analyst_take: AppLovin passes all six framework checks with extraordinary Rule of 40 (143). The 36% drawdown from highs combined with declining shorts and 33% analyst upside makes this a high-conviction BUY despite the 22.5x forward P/E premium.

growth_picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 84.7% — best in this set, far above 30%.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 325% >> revenue growth 84.7% — extreme operating leverage.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 32.59% — clears 20% bar with negligible debt-to-equity (2.48) and $8.0B cash.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.48% (<5%, qualifies on level) BUT rising 1.06% MoM — fails the AND criterion, qualifies as borderline (<10%).

ai_exposure.detail: AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) is the explicit growth engine; commercial wins driven directly by AI workflow adoption.

deal_radar.assessment: Net cash $7.8B (cash $8.03B vs debt $0.21B) — fortress balance sheet; current ratio 6.91. FCF $1.75B; capital returns optionality high.

catalysts_general: Down 34.51% from 52-week high; 20d SMA ($140.76) and 50d SMA ($145.51) above spot — short-term downtrend.
Analyst mean target $181.14 implies 33.3% upside despite mixed analyst distribution (1 strong buy, 17 buy, 11 hold, 1 sell, 1 strong sell).
Forward P/E 67.55 vs trailing 154.4 — earnings re-rating priced in.
P/S 62.4 and P/B 44.0 — extreme multiples; any growth deceleration carries large multiple-compression risk.
PEG 2.9 — expensive even adjusted for growth.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Continued grind toward 52w low region; multiple compression with no fundamental change.
Retest 52-week low on AI-spend slowdown narrative.
Growth decel to ~50%; multiple resets toward growth-software median.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near 52-week low ($107); forward P/E compresses to ~55x — still rich, but offers asymmetric upside to $181 mean target if growth holds.

comparables.note: Data-software peer; PLTR trades at premium multiple even after drawdown.
Hyper-growth software comparable; PLTR's 84% growth vs more mature CRWD justifies some premium but not the full gap.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Palantir is the strongest fundamentals story in this set with 5.5/6 framework score and triple-digit earnings acceleration. The 67.5x forward P/E and rising short interest hold it back from BUY — WATCHLIST until either a deeper drawdown toward 52w low or another quarter of growth confirmation.

growth_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 55.5% — extraordinary for a $72B-revenue pharma; well above 30%.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 169.9% > revenue growth 55.5% — strong operating leverage on GLP-1 ramp.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 107.5% — clears 20% bar; flattered by 139 D/E but supported by $9.2B FCF.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 0.91% (<5% level qualifies for pass) BUT rising 18.06% MoM — fails AND condition, borderline on '<10%'.

ai_exposure.detail: AI accelerates drug discovery, trial design, and patient stratification — modest near-term tailwind on R&D efficiency.

deal_radar.assessment: Net debt $38.1B (debt $43.4B vs cash $5.3B) — heavy investment in manufacturing capacity (Mounjaro/Zepbound supply); FCF $9.2B services it but limits flexibility. Capex-heavy profile inferred from balance sheet.

catalysts_general: Down 12.79% from 52-week high; analyst mean target $1,203.90 implies 21.7% upside.
Forward P/E 22.6 vs trailing 35.1 — earnings expected to grow ~55% to $43.76 forward EPS.
Strong analyst distribution: 24 buy/strong-buy out of 31 (rec mean 1.71).
Beta 0.48 — defensive characteristics versus other growth names in this set.
Short interest spike 18.06% MoM (small base 0.91%) — emerging skeptic activity worth monitoring.

downside_scenarios.scenario: GLP-1 competition concern (Novo, MNK) + multiple compression.
Manufacturing miss or trial setback; reverts to 50d/200d region.
GLP-1 demand cools or pricing pressure; closer to 52w low ($623.78).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near current 20-day SMA ($921.51); forward P/E ~21x with 30%+ upside to mean target — best risk/reward entry without waiting for a major drawdown.

comparables.note: Novo Nordisk — direct GLP-1 competitor; LLY's 55.5% revenue growth and 169.9% earnings growth dominate.
Pharma comparable; LLY commands premium given GLP-1 franchise scale.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Lilly delivers 5.5/6 with mega-cap-defying 55% revenue growth and 170% earnings growth. The 18% MoM rise in short interest off a tiny base is the only blemish — BUY with sizing discipline given the elevated multiple and rising bear positioning.

growth_picks.name: Visa Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 17.1% — below 30% pass; in 15-30% borderline band.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margin 97.78% — among the highest disclosed; payment-network economics.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 35.5% > revenue growth 17.1% — operating leverage from network scale.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 1.41% (<5%) AND declining 12.07% — clean tape.

ai_exposure.detail: AI enhances fraud detection and risk modeling — efficiency gains, not transformative; agentic-commerce optionality is plausible but unproven in numbers.

deal_radar.assessment: Net debt $10B (debt $24B vs cash $13.9B); FCF $20.8B = 3.4% FCF yield on $612B cap. Payout ratio 22% leaves substantial buyback firepower.

catalysts_general: Down 14.24% from 52-week high but above 20d ($315.22) and 50d ($311.07) SMAs — short-term uptrend.
Analyst mean target $398.31 implies 23.7% upside; lowest target ($323) right at spot.
Strongest analyst conviction in set: 35 buy/strong-buy out of 38, rec mean 1.34.
Forward P/E 21.72 vs trailing 28.10 — modest multiple compression with growth.
Short interest down 12.07% MoM — bears capitulating.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Consumer-spend slowdown narrative; tests 52w low region.
Retest of 52-week low on regulatory/interchange-fee headlines.
Severe consumer recession + multiple compression; structural threat from stablecoin rails.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near 50-day SMA ($311) with forward P/E ~21x — gives ~28% upside to mean target while preserving the network-economics moat.

comparables.note: Mastercard — direct duopoly peer; typically trades at slight premium to V on growth optics.
American Express — different model (issuer); V's network-only economics show in 97.8% gross margin vs AXP.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Visa is the lowest-revenue-growth name here (17.1%, borderline) but compensates with 97.8% gross margin, 60% ROE, and the strongest analyst conviction (1.34 mean rec) of the set. BUY as the defensive-quality compounder anchor — modest upside, low downside variance.

growth_picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 73.2% — well above 30% on a $216B revenue base.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 95.6% > revenue growth 73.2% — operating leverage continuing despite scale.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 101.5% — far above 20% bar; debt-to-equity only 7.26 with $62.6B cash.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 1.22% (<5% level passes) BUT rising 23.6% MoM — significant bear-positioning increase fails the AND condition; borderline at '<10%'.

ai_exposure.detail: Defining AI infrastructure beneficiary; data-center accelerated computing is the core revenue driver.

deal_radar.assessment: Net cash $51.1B (cash $62.6B vs debt $11.4B); FCF $58.1B = 1.2% yield on $4.78T cap. Capital returns option huge but constrained by R&D pace.

catalysts_general: Down only 9.38% from all-time high; outperformer among this group on 90-day basis (+12.81%).
Forward P/E 17.48 with PEG 0.63 — cheapest growth-adjusted valuation in this set.
Analyst mean target $269.17 implies 37.0% upside; conviction extreme (57 of 60 buy/strong-buy/hold favorable, rec mean 1.28).
Short interest UP 23.6% MoM — meaningful bear build, even if low absolute (1.22%); worth tracking.
Forward EPS $11.24 vs trailing $4.89 — earnings power more than doubling.

downside_scenarios.scenario: AI-capex digestion narrative; reverts to 200-day range.
Hyperscaler capex pause + multiple compression; analyst low target $140.
Retest 52-week low on China/export-restriction shock or AI-bubble unwind.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Mild pullback to 50-day SMA ($187) zone with PEG ~0.6 — provides 50%+ upside to mean target while monitoring rising-shorts signal.

comparables.note: Closest GPU/accelerator peer; NVDA's 71% gross margin and 65% operating margin dwarf AMD.
AI-infrastructure adjacent; NVDA's growth and margin profile remain superior.

growth_picks.analyst_take: NVIDIA passes 5.5/6 with the lowest valuation in this set on a PEG basis (0.63) and 37% upside to mean target. The 23.6% MoM jump in short interest is the one yellow flag worth watching — BUY but size to accept volatility, ideally on a pullback toward the 50-day.
